mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate spikes above 4.5% within a single month, it signals rapid labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity drawdowns over the next 5 trading days due to accelerated recession pricing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d forward returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of credit availability and housing demand drives equity risk premiums. When credit supply contracts while housing demand remains elevated, it indicates financial stress without real economy support, pressuring SPX over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5% month-over-month while HOUST remains above 1300
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS/HOUST signal fails to predict negative 60d returns in >50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During high-inflation environments, supply constraints become the dominant pricing factor. A rising industrial production index signals easing bottlenecks and margin recovery, supporting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not correlate with positive 20d SPX returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real GDP reflects expanding corporate earnings potential and aggregate demand strength, which systematically lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60d SPX returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, the cost of capital compresses equity multiples. A subsequent monthly decline of at least 0.25 percentage points triggers a relief rally in SPX over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% followed by a monthly decline of at least 0.25 percentage points
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns are not significantly positive following FEDFUNDS cuts above the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between employment growth and unemployment stability drives equity risk premiums. When payrolls rise month-over-month while unemployment remains below 4.0%, it confirms a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20d returns in >55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a declining demand regime where real GDP growth turns negative, credit availability acts as a leading indicator of liquidity stress. A sharp drop in credit supply under these conditions predicts negative SPX returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below zero
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during negative GDPC1 growth periods do not lead to negative 60d SPX returns in >60% of cases.
